claim: Thigh-length graduated compression stockings (GCS) did not reduce deep vein thrombosis in patients admitted to hospital who are immobile because of acute stroke.

result.claim: Thigh-length graduated compression stockings (GCS) did not reduce deep vein thrombosis in patients admitted to hospital who are immobile because of acute stroke.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from a multicentre, randomised controlled trial (CLOTS trial 1, Paper ID: 11718220). This study, involving 2518 patients admitted to hospital with acute stroke, found no significant reduction in deep vein thrombosis (DVT) with the use of thigh-length graduated compression stockings (GCS). Specifically, the primary outcome (occurrence of symptomatic or asymptomatic DVT) was 10.0% in the GCS group and 10.5% in the group avoiding GCS, an absolute risk reduction of 0.5% (95% CI -1.9% to 2.9%), which was not statistically significant. The study concluded that these data do not support the use of thigh-length GCS in this patient population. Furthermore, the study reported significantly more skin breaks, ulcers, blisters, and skin necrosis in patients allocated to GCS.

evidence.text: The objective of the study was to determine whether statin use is associated with reduced hip fracture risk.
evidence.source: HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors and the risk of hip fractures in elderly patients.

evidence.text: Skin breaks, ulcers, blisters, and skin necrosis were significantly more common in patients allocated to GCS than in those allocated to avoid their use.
evidence.source: Effectiveness of thigh-length graduated compression stockings to reduce the risk of deep vein thrombosis after stroke (CLOTS trial 1): a multicentre, randomised controlled trial

evidence.text: Patients without established atherothrombosis but with risk factors only (n = 8073) had the lowest risk of subsequent ischemic events (9.1%; 95% CI, 8.3%-9.9%).
evidence.source: Comparative determinants of 4-year cardiovascular event rates in stable outpatients at risk of or with atherothrombosis.

evidence.text: The study included patients who received red blood cell transfusions following repair of hip fracture.
evidence.source: Clinical outcomes following institution of the Canadian universal leukoreduction program for red blood cell transfusions.

evidence.text: Compression Doppler ultrasound of both legs was undertaken again at 25-30 days after enrolment, when practical.
evidence.source: Effectiveness of thigh-length graduated compression stockings to reduce the risk of deep vein thrombosis after stroke (CLOTS trial 1): a multicentre, randomised controlled trial

evidence.text: The odds ratio for skin breaks, ulcers, blisters, and skin necrosis was 4.18 in patients allocated to GCS, compared to those allocated to avoid GCS use.
evidence.source: Effectiveness of thigh-length graduated compression stockings to reduce the risk of deep vein thrombosis after stroke (CLOTS trial 1): a multicentre, randomised controlled trial

evidence.text: The primary outcome occurred in 133 (10.5%) patients allocated to avoid GCS.

evidence.text: 23 072 nondiabetic patients had no survival benefit with platelet GP IIb/IIIa inhibition.

evidence.text: Transmural LGE remained independent after adjustment for N-terminal pro-brain natriuretic peptide, ejection fraction, stroke volume index, E/E', and left ventricular mass index with a hazard ratio of 4.1.
